content.text:
```json
{
  "sources": [
    {
      "title": "Schubert's Impromptus: Formal Design and the Problem of Repetition",
      "author": "Charles Fisk",
      "year": 1993,
      "type": "academic",
      "publication": "19th-Century Music, Vol. 17, No. 2",
      "url": "https://www.jstor.org/stable/746378",
      "description": "A peer-reviewed analytical study examining the formal structures and repetition strategies across Schubert's Impromptus Op. 90, with focused attention on the G-flat major work's ternary design and its implications for performance and interpretation."
    },
    {
      "title": "Returning Cycles: Contexts for the Interpretation of Schubert's Impromptus and Last Sonatas",
      "author": "Charles Fisk",
      "year": 2001,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "University of California Press",
      "url": "https://www.ucpress.edu/book/9780520221581/returning-cycles",
      "description": "An extended academic monograph offering in-depth hermeneutic and structural analysis of the Op. 90 Impromptus, including the G-flat major Impromptu, situating the works within Schubert's late style and exploring tonal narrative, memory, and cyclic return."
    },
    {
      "title": "Schubert: The Piano Music",
      "author": "Charles Osborne",
      "year": 1988,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "Gollancz",
      "url": "https://www.worldcat.org/title/schubert-the-piano-music/oclc/18026535",
      "description": "A comprehensive critical survey of Schubert's piano output that includes substantial analytical commentary on the Impromptu in G-flat major D.899 No. 3, addressing its lyrical character, textural features, and place within the Op. 90 set."
    }
  ]
}
```
